programmes.show_description: Jay deals with a couple who are obsessed with getting cheap tattoos on holiday. Lou sorts out Thom, who has a rude tribute to one of his friends on his rear end.
Water, sand and beach balls contribute a seaside feel to this episode, but the jovial tone is soured by one contestant's flagrant cheating.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Phil Wang take on guest captain Russell Howard and Roisin Conaty. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
TGI Fridays is one of the biggest restaurant chains. CEO Ray Blanchette wants to re-energise the brand, so he goes undercover to find out what staff think of his changes.
Xavier Mufraggi, the CEO of Club Med North America, goes undercover, disguised as a laid-back surfer, in his own resorts, to see the impact that his growth plans are having.
In Essex, sports car manufacturers Charlotte and Neil gear up for some tip-top cuisine. Newly-weds Luke and Alice plan a panto theme. And Hardeep and Sareena host a 'Bhangra Bonanza'.
In Aberdeen, Leanne and Calvin's Ibizan feast competes with Gail and Iain's posh nosh, and Becca and Grant's vegetarian fare, in a week featuring spooky stories and a near-marital-meltdown!
...Selling: Sunita and David's time constraints mean it's not ideal when Jonathan finds a dreadful discovery. Sunita realises the sacrifices she must make to buy a cheaper home.
The Aussie Soap One: Australia-born Adam is taking a risk planning an '80s Aussie soap' wedding when his bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated...
The One Under Edinburgh: Jack plans a haunting, horror-themed wedding, while his bride-to-be Becka dreams of a traditional Scottish affair. Will Jack's concept be a screaming success?
Warren Boone, the chief people officer of restaurant chain Coco's Bakery, steps off the corporate ladder to get a taste of life on the company's front line.
Mayor of Fontana, Acquanetta Warren, goes undercover in the Californian city to spend time with sewer workers and the fire brigade to see how the city is recovering from Covid.
Return to Amy's Baking Company: Gordon revisits the most contentious restaurant he's ever tried to rescue - Amy's Baking Company, in Scottsdale, Arizona.
Pantaleone's: In Colorado, Gordon visits Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, whose stubborn owner Pete claims that he makes 'the best pizza in Denver'. Sadly, Gordon doesn't agree.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Nyahsa and Hezekiyah's hopes of fixing up their home quickly turned into a nightmare. Can Jonathan and Drew dream up an innovative solution?
Hurricane Bonnie: Captain Glenn reprimands the crew over recent slip-ups. And Chef Ileisha is determined to prove she can complete a 10-course dinner.
There's eraser eating, camera wrecking and caravan crushing in the final episode of the series. Which of our intrepid comics will emerge triumphant?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams. David Baddiel joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Mr Morris Returns: The family are distraught when Grandma gets engaged to Mr Morris, her horrible 85-year-old boyfriend. And Jim collects money for charity in full dog costume.
The Anniversary: Mum and Dad's anniversary is ruined when Dad paints a picture of Mum that looks like Margaret Thatcher. Jim goes sleep-walking.
Victoria is in desperate need of a tattoo cover-up so she can train as a teacher. Abigail gets inked at the age of 75 and Hollie loses her cheeky chicken tattoo.
There's eraser eating, camera wrecking and caravan crushing in the final episode of the series. Which of our intrepid comics will emerge triumphant?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams. David Baddiel joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
As CEO, Wan Kim has doubled Smoothie King's number of stores and tripled sales. He goes undercover to find out what his changes have meant for his employees and customers.
Colie Edison created every element of the bowling giant Bowlero. Now she goes undercover to find out how the brand is working for staff and customers.
In East Anglia, there's collapsing food and cheeky shortcuts, as competitive Kim and Martin, landscape gardeners Debbie and Richard, and gadget gurus Jamie and Mark compete.
In Plymouth, there's a Chris crisis as half of the rival diners share the same name! Will a locally sourced supper, celebrity fancy dress or a nautical theme win the week?
...Selling: Eric and Misty's DIY projects cause Jonathan some housing havoc. Before Drew can find their next place, the couple must prioritise agreeing on their priorities.
The Big Ibizan Zoo One: Sonny is a party fanatic. How will his bride Jazmin greet his plans for a zoo-themed Ibizan beach party wedding with everyone covered in animal-print body paint?
The One in the Sky: The sky's the limit for adrenaline junkie Jai who plans to have a wedding in the sky, even though his bride-to-be Leanne is terrified of heights.
Linda L. Chadwick, CEO of Rita's Italian Ice & Frozen Custard, goes undercover to find out how her new initiatives are working and to help franchisees become more profitable.
Andrew Simon, CEO of Fremont Street Experience, who started at the Las Vegas attraction at the start of the pandemic, goes undercover to discover and address issues.
Old Neighborhood: Gordon visits Arvada in Colorado, where out-of-control hoarder Randy and his wife Alexa have allowed their restaurant Old Neighborhood to fall into disrepair.
Kati Allo: Gordon's in New York, where the stress of running a Greek restaurant has driven Manny and Christine to the brink of divorce, with their daughter stuck in the middle.
...and Selling: Brand new series: It's convention vs contemporary when Krystal and Cam can't agree on their new home. The twins help with compromises and costly constructions.
She Loves Me Not: Captain Glenn is proud of the crew and their pride charter, but he also has bad news for them. And Gary hears about Mads' three-way kiss.
David Baddiel, Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo make very dramatic entrances, as Greg Davies returns, dragging Little Alex Horne behind him.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and James Acaster take on Lee Mack and Sarah Millican, with John Cooper Clarke in Dictionary Corner.
Jimmy Carr hosts the panel/quiz show mash-up. Jon Richardson and Chris Addison go up against Joe Wilkinson and Katherine Ryan, with David O'Doherty in Dictionary Corner.
The Piano: Dad has bought a piano, and the world's most sarcastic piano tuner comes round to tune it. Meanwhile, the family spot Aunty Val having an affair. What should they do?
The Big Day: It's Grandma's wedding day, and she's getting married to the horrible Mr Morris. The family are distraught, but will Grandma really say 'I do'?
